question: A bank mandates an external firm to handle a function significant to its business as a one-off project lasting three months. Does this arrangement qualify as outsourcing under FINMA's outsourcing requirements?

A bank hands the operation of its staff cafeteria to an external caterer; the activity has no bearing on compliance with financial market legislation. Is this a significant function within the meaning of FINMA's outsourcing rules?
Is a Swiss branch of a foreign securities firm covered by FINMA's outsourcing requirements?
A foreign insurer's Swiss branch required authorisation under the Insurance Supervision Act to commence business operations. Does FINMA's outsourcing circular apply to that branch?
Are managers of collective assets with their registered office in Switzerland subject to FINMA's outsourcing requirements?
What applies if a SICAV administers itself rather than delegating its administration — does it fall within the scope of FINMA's outsourcing circular?
Leaving aside the functions expressly reserved as exceptions, may a supervised company outsource any of its significant functions?
Do insurance captives enjoy more latitude than other insurance companies when it comes to outsourcing management and control functions?

A reinsurance captive domiciled in Switzerland wants to hand over its entire management, including central executive management functions, to a firm appropriately specialised in running captives. Is this admissible?
May a fund management company delegate the decision on the issue of fund units to an external service provider?
section: C. Managers of collective assets, fund management companies and

question: In which internal document set do securities firms keep their inventory of outsourced functions?
section: V. Requirements for outsourcing companies / A. Inventory of outsourced functions

question: Which type of analysis, weighing the main economic and operational considerations, must be carried out before an outsourcing agreement is signed?
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: With regard to continuity of the outsourced service, what must the service provider be able to guarantee?
accept: a guarantee of permanent service provision
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: A bank is negotiating an outsourcing contract. Must arrangements be in place for the function to be taken back in-house or moved to another provider in an orderly manner?
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: Must the division of duties between the outsourcing company and its service provider — in particular interfaces and responsibilities — be fixed contractually?
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: Into which of the company's systems must an outsourced function be integrated?
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: Once a function has been outsourced, what must the company do systematically with the main associated risks? Name the four required actions.
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: Which two rights over the service provider must the outsourcing agreement secure for the outsourcing company?
section: B. Selection, instruction and monitoring of the service provider

question: A bank plans to outsource the operation of security-relevant IT infrastructure. Must specific security requirements be agreed in the contract with the service provider?
What applies if a planned outsourcing would impede FINMA's supervision of the outsourced function — is such an arrangement admissible?

A company outsources a significant function to a provider that is not itself supervised by FINMA. Must that provider contractually commit to supplying FINMA with all information and documentation needed for its supervisory activities?
In which country must access to the information required for a potential restructuring or resolution of a supervised company be possible at all times?
A company and its service provider settle the terms of an outsourcing purely verbally, without any documented record. Does this satisfy FINMA's requirements for the form of the outsourcing agreement?
The provider of a significant outsourced function intends to replace one of its subcontractors. Must the outsourcing company be informed of this at an early stage?
Which two internal matters relating to the initiation and conclusion of outsourcing projects must a company specify?
Can FINMA, in justified cases, release a company entirely from complying with the outsourcing circular?

A bank authorised under Article 1b of the Banking Act is preparing its outsourcing risk analysis. May it determine within that analysis whether the requirements on selecting and instructing the service provider are relevant and applicable to it?
Must an institution under Article 1b of the Banking Act make provision for taking outsourced functions back in-house?

Institutions falling under Articles 47a–47e of the Capital Adequacy Ordinance may implement their duty to monitor and assess the service provider on an ongoing basis in an alternative way — by what means?
gold: regular reporting by an independent auditor
accept: regular reports from an independent auditor

question: An insurance company communicates a change to its business plan to FINMA for approval. Does the outsourcing circular apply to this authorisation for changes from that point in time?
